title: Implement structured logging with automatic trace context inclusion
purpose: Provide a structured logging facility that automatically includes the active trace context in every log entry. All log output is structured key-value pairs — never free-form strings. This enables operators to search logs by trace_id or span_id and correlate log entries with distributed traces. The logging layer reads the current TraceContext from OBS-006 and injects trace_id and span_id fields into every emitted record.
task: Create crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs containing the structured logging types and the LogEntry struct. Define LogLevel, LogEntry (with timestamp, level, message, trace context, and arbitrary key-value fields), and a LogEmitter trait for output sinks. Provide a global log function that captures the current trace context automatically. All entries are structured — no free-form string interpolation in the logging API.

requirements.title: Define LogLevel enum
requirements.spec: LogLevel SHALL have exactly five variants: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error, ordered by increasing severity. LogLevel SHALL derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord. LogLevel SHALL NOT include Fatal or Off — those are not log levels.
acceptance: LogLevel has exactly five variants: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error
LogLevel derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord
LogLevel::Trace < LogLevel::Debug < LogLevel::Info < LogLevel::Warn < LogLevel::Error

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — define the new logging module and keep it under the 500-line cap; tests can live at the bottom if total size remains safe
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:14-29 — exact LogLevel variants, derive list, and ordering acceptance
crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1-9 — current tracing module style; only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports
Cargo.toml:18-27 — workspace lints deny clippy all/pedantic, unwrap_used, expect_used, and panic
context: No `logging.rs` exists yet under `crates/liminal/src/tracing`; this brief creates it rather than extending span/context files.

Enums in nearby tracing code derive only what they need: `TraceContext` derives `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq`; `FinishedSpan` derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`; follow explicit derives rather than adding extras.
Variant order alone can satisfy `Trace < Debug < Info < Warn < Error`; do not add discriminants unless needed, and do not add `Fatal` or `Off`.
Because public types must implement Debug and clippy pedantic is denied, add unit tests for ordering and avoid unused public helpers.
scout.approach: Create `pub enum LogLevel { Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error }` in the new logging module with exactly `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]`. Add a small test asserting the ordering chain. Keep this definition near the top of `logging.rs` so later builder/emitter code can share it.
scout.notes: The crate currently depends on the external `tracing` crate in `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml`, but the brief boundary says not to use it for this API; implement `LogLevel` in `crate::tracing::logging`, not via the dependency.

files_changed.note: Defined LogLevel with exactly Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error in increasing severity order and required derives.

dev.how: Added `LogLevel` near the top of the new logging module with the exact five variants and `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord` derives. Unit coverage asserts the severity ordering chain.

checklist.note: The log level model only defines severity values; structured-entry data is represented separately through typed fields, not free-form levels or extra text channels.

acceptance.criterion: LogLevel has exactly five variants: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:18-25 defines LogLevel with only Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error.

acceptance.criterion: LogLevel derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:18 derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord.

acceptance.criterion: LogLevel::Trace < LogLevel::Debug < LogLevel::Info < LogLevel::Warn < LogLevel::Error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:270-276 test tracing::logging::tests::log_levels_are_ordered_by_severity asserts the ordering; cargo test -p liminal reported this test ok.

requirements.title: Define LogEntry as a structured key-value record
requirements.spec: LogEntry SHALL contain: timestamp (Instant), level (LogLevel), target (module path as &'static str), message (String), trace_context (Option<TraceContext>), and fields (Vec<(String, LogValue)>). LogEntry SHALL implement Debug. LogValue SHALL be an enum supporting String, i64, u64, f64, and bool variants. LogEntry SHALL NOT contain any unstructured text field beyond message — all additional data goes in fields.
acceptance: LogEntry has fields: timestamp, level, target, message, trace_context, fields
LogEntry implements Debug
LogValue has variants: String, I64, U64, F64, Bool
No free-form text field exists beyond the message field

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — add `LogValue` and `LogEntry` here alongside `LogLevel`
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:3-39 — `TraceContext` is Copy/Debug and exposes `trace_id()`/`span_id()` accessors for log searchability
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:30-47 — exact required fields and `LogValue` variants
Cargo.toml:18-27 — no panics/unwraps/expects in production; tests are allowed but still run under denied lints unless locally justified
context: `LogEntry` should contain exactly the required structural fields: `timestamp: Instant`, `level: LogLevel`, `target: &'static str`, `message: String`, `trace_context: Option<TraceContext>`, and `fields: Vec<(String, LogValue)>`.

`LogValue` variants should be exactly `String(String)`, `I64(i64)`, `U64(u64)`, `F64(f64)`, and `Bool(bool)`; deriving `Clone`, `Debug`, and `PartialEq` will make tests and emitters easier while satisfying Debug.
The design says no extra free-form text beyond `message`; avoid fields such as `text`, `body`, `details`, or `args`.
Use `std::time::Instant`, matching span timing code in `tracing/span.rs:9` and tests around `span.start()`.
scout.approach: Define `LogValue` and `LogEntry` in `logging.rs`. Prefer public fields if acceptance checks are likely to construct/read entries directly, or provide accessors in addition if encapsulation is desired; either way, preserve the exact field names. `LogEntry` can derive `Debug` and likely `Clone`/`PartialEq` if tests need comparisons, though `Instant` supports these. Add tests that construct a `LogEntry` and pattern-match each `LogValue` variant.
scout.notes: `f64` prevents deriving `Eq` for `LogValue`; do not try to satisfy the global public-Debug convention by over-deriving traits that `f64` cannot support.

files_changed.note: Added LogValue and LogEntry with the required fields and Debug implementations.

dev.how: Added `LogValue` with exactly `String`, `I64`, `U64`, `F64`, and `Bool` variants, plus `LogEntry` containing `timestamp`, `level`, `target`, `message`, `trace_context`, and `fields`. `LogEntry` derives Debug and has no additional free-form text field beyond `message`; all extra data is the typed `Vec<(String, LogValue)>`.

checklist.note: Additional log data is modeled only as typed key-value pairs in `fields`; no extra free-form string body/details field was added.

stories.note: Entries carry optional trace context and expose trace/span ID accessors for searching logs by conversation.

acceptance.criterion: LogEntry has fields: timestamp, level, target, message, trace_context, fields
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:36-44 defines LogEntry with timestamp, level, target, message, trace_context, and fields.

acceptance.criterion: LogEntry implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:36 derives Debug for LogEntry; test tracing::logging::tests::public_logging_types_are_debug passed in cargo test -p liminal.

acceptance.criterion: LogValue has variants: String, I64, U64, F64, Bool
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:27-34 defines LogValue with String, I64, U64, F64, Bool.

acceptance.criterion: No free-form text field exists beyond the message field
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:36-44 shows the only text field on LogEntry is message; extra data is fields: Vec<(String, LogValue)>.

requirements.title: Automatic trace context inclusion in log entries
requirements.spec: When a log entry is created within a conversation context, the active TraceContext SHALL be automatically captured and included in the LogEntry's trace_context field. The trace_id and span_id SHALL be available as searchable fields. When no trace context is active, trace_context SHALL be None. Trace context capture SHALL NOT require the caller to pass the context explicitly.
acceptance: A log entry created within a conversation has trace_context == Some(ctx)
A log entry created outside a conversation has trace_context == None
trace_id and span_id from the trace context are accessible on the LogEntry
No explicit TraceContext parameter is required at the log call site

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — `LogEntryBuilder::build()` and any log emission function should call `current_trace_context()` automatically
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 — public `current_trace_context()` reads the existing thread-local active trace context
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:187-267 — `SpanGuard` sets/restores active context when conversation spans are entered/dropped
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:31-39 — accessors for trace_id/span_id that can be exposed/searchable without duplicating context state
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:49-65 — acceptance criteria for Some(ctx), None, searchable IDs, and no explicit parameter
context: The thread-local active context is already implemented by OBS-006 in `span.rs`; logging should read it rather than define a second TLS store.
`current_trace_context()` returns `Option<TraceContext>` by value because `TraceContext` is Copy, so capture is cheap and lock-free.
`SpanGuard::start_conversation` is the test harness to create an active conversation context; dropping the guard restores `None`.

Searchable IDs can be satisfied either through `entry.trace_context.map(|ctx| ctx.trace_id())`/`span_id()` or by adding convenience accessors on `LogEntry`; avoid duplicating `trace_id`/`span_id` into the `fields` vector unless tests/design explicitly require it.
scout.approach: Make `LogEntryBuilder::build()` populate `trace_context` from `crate::tracing::current_trace_context()` with no context parameter in `new()` or `field()`. Add tests that build outside a span and assert `None`, then build inside `let guard = SpanGuard::start_conversation("...")` and assert `Some(guard.context())`. Add `LogEntry::trace_id()` and `LogEntry::span_id()` convenience methods returning `Option<u128>`/`Option<u64>` if you want the “searchable fields” acceptance to be direct without changing the required struct shape.
scout.notes: Do not import or use the external `tracing` crate despite the crate dependency; the module path name overlaps and can confuse imports. Use `crate::tracing::{current_trace_context, TraceContext}`.

files_changed.note: Made LogEntryBuilder::build capture the active TraceContext automatically and added trace/span ID accessors.

dev.how: `LogEntryBuilder::build()` reads `crate::tracing::current_trace_context()` through a private helper and stores the result in `LogEntry::trace_context`, requiring no explicit context parameter at construction call sites. `LogEntry::trace_id()` and `LogEntry::span_id()` make the captured IDs directly accessible.

checklist.note: Structured log entries include the active trace context when OBS-006 span TLS has one available.

stories.note: Application code can build log entries inside spans without manually passing TraceContext.
Trace and span IDs are searchable through the captured context/accessors on each entry.

requirements.title: Thread-local trace context storage for automatic capture
requirements.spec: The current TraceContext SHALL be stored in a thread-local variable that is set when a conversation span begins and cleared when it ends. The log entry constructor SHALL read this thread-local to populate trace_context. This mechanism SHALL be transparent to application code. SHALL NOT use a global Mutex for context storage — thread-local is lock-free.
acceptance: A thread-local TraceContext is set when entering a conversation span
The thread-local is cleared when leaving the conversation span
Log entries automatically read the thread-local for trace context
No Mutex or RwLock is used for context storage

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:17-19 — existing `thread_local!` `ACTIVE_TRACE_CONTEXT: Cell<Option<TraceContext>>` storage
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:230-257 — entering a `SpanGuard` replaces TLS with `Some(context)` and records previous context
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:261-267 — `Drop` restores previous context when leaving a span
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 — existing public read accessor plus private replace helper
crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — read the existing accessor only; do not create Mutex/RwLock context storage here
context: R4’s storage requirement is already met by OBS-006: a thread-local `Cell<Option<TraceContext>>` is set/cleared by `SpanGuard`.
The current implementation supports nesting by storing `previous_context` and restoring it, so logging tests should include nested spans only if needed, not reimplement nesting.
The acceptance says no Mutex/RwLock for context storage; `logging.rs` should not add any context-specific synchronization at all.
`metrics/registry.rs` uses RwLock for metrics state, but that pattern is not appropriate for trace context; avoid copying it for R4.
scout.approach: Treat `span.rs` as the owner of context storage. In `logging.rs`, add a small private helper such as `fn capture_trace_context() -> Option<TraceContext> { current_trace_context() }` if useful, and have builders call it during `build()`. Tests for R4 should exercise `SpanGuard` rather than exposing new setters: start a guard, build an entry, assert context; drop guard, build another entry, assert None; optionally assert source contains no context Mutex/RwLock through code review rather than runtime behavior.
scout.notes: The brief says modify `logging.rs` for R4, but the actual set/clear implementation is already in `span.rs`. Avoid moving TLS from `span.rs`; moving it would create churn and risk breaking OBS-006 behavior.

files_changed.note: Read the existing thread-local trace context accessor from span.rs without adding Mutex/RwLock storage.

dev.how: Kept OBS-006 as the owner of thread-local context storage and made logging read `current_trace_context()` during entry construction. Tests exercise entering and leaving `SpanGuard` scopes to confirm entries capture `Some(ctx)` inside a conversation and `None` after the guard drops.

checklist.note: Log construction transparently observes the active thread-local trace context.

stories.note: No application-visible context plumbing was added for intra-bus logging.

acceptance.criterion: A thread-local TraceContext is set when entering a conversation span
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:17-19 declares thread_local ACTIVE_TRACE_CONTEXT; crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:230-235 sets it to Some(context) when SpanGuard enters a conversation.

acceptance.criterion: The thread-local is cleared when leaving the conversation span
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:253-267 restores previous_context on finish/drop; test tracing::span::tests::span_guard_sets_and_restores_current_context passed in cargo test -p liminal.

acceptance.criterion: Log entries automatically read the thread-local for trace context
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:120-128 build() calls capture_trace_context(); crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:234-235 reads current_trace_context().

acceptance.criterion: No Mutex or RwLock is used for context storage
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:17-19 uses thread_local Cell<Option<TraceContext>>; targeted search over crates/liminal/src/tracing for Mutex|RwLock returned no Mutex/RwLock matches after hardening.

requirements.title: Define LogEmitter trait for output sinks
requirements.spec: LogEmitter SHALL be a trait with a single method: emit(entry: &LogEntry). This is the extension point for log output destinations (stderr, file, network). A NoopEmitter SHALL be provided that discards all entries. The active emitter SHALL be set globally via an atomic pointer. When no emitter is installed, logging SHALL be a no-op with zero overhead. LogEmitter SHALL require Send + Sync.
acceptance: LogEmitter trait has fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry)
NoopEmitter implements LogEmitter and discards entries
A global optional LogEmitter is set via atomic pointer
When no emitter is installed, log calls perform no work
LogEmitter: Send + Sync is required

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — define `LogEmitter`, `NoopEmitter`, global emitter storage, install/global accessors, and emission function
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:13-15 — existing atomic marker + `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>` pattern for global sink install
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:270-342 — `SpanCollector`, `NoopCollector`, install error, enabled fast path, and global accessor pattern to mirror
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:184-209 — alternative global registry pattern using an atomic enabled flag before accessing `OnceLock`
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:85-102 — exact emitter trait and no-op requirements
context: The span collector already uses `AtomicPtr<()>` as a cheap installed marker plus `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>`; this is the closest precedent for the required global optional `LogEmitter` atomic pointer behavior.

`SpanCollector` is `std::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync + 'static`; `LogEmitter` must at least be `Debug + Send + Sync + 'static` if you want all public types and trait objects to satisfy Debug conventions.
When no emitter is installed, the hot log call should return before constructing `LogEntry` or allocating fields; the fast path should be a single atomic load/null check.
OnceLock means installation can be one-shot; if you expose install functions, define a Debug error type implementing `Display` and `Error`, mirroring `SpanCollectorInstallError`.
scout.approach: Define `pub trait LogEmitter: std::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync + 'static { fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry); }` and `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)] pub struct NoopEmitter;`. For global storage, mirror `SPAN_COLLECTOR_MARKER`/`GLOBAL_SPAN_COLLECTOR`: an atomic marker/pointer for enabled check and a `OnceLock<Box<dyn LogEmitter>>` for ownership. Provide `install_log_emitter`/`install_boxed_log_emitter`, `log_emitter_enabled()`, `global_log_emitter()`, and an emission API that checks enabled before building/emitting.
scout.notes: A literal atomic pointer to the trait object is hard because fat pointers are not directly atomic. The existing code solves this with an atomic thin marker plus `OnceLock` for the actual trait object; use that convention unless acceptance tests demand a differently named atomic. Do not make `NoopEmitter` the default installed emitter, because “no emitter installed” must be a true no-op fast path.

files_changed.note: Added LogEmitter, NoopEmitter, install helpers, global emitter state, and fast enabled checks.

dev.how: Defined `LogEmitter: Debug + Send + Sync + 'static` with the single required `emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry)` method and provided `NoopEmitter`. Global installation mirrors the existing span collector pattern: `OnceLock<Box<dyn LogEmitter>>` owns the emitter while an atomic pointer marker provides the cheap installed check; absent emitters cause `log`/`log_with_fields` to return before constructing an entry.

checklist.note: Emitters receive fully structured `LogEntry` values and the core does not add formatting or free-form output.

acceptance.criterion: LogEmitter trait has fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:64-66 defines LogEmitter with fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry).

acceptance.criterion: NoopEmitter implements LogEmitter and discards entries
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:68-75 implements LogEmitter for NoopEmitter and does no work with the entry; test tracing::logging::tests::noop_emitter_discards_entries passed.

acceptance.criterion: A global optional LogEmitter is set via atomic pointer
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in harden pass: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:12 declares static LOG_EMITTER_PTR: AtomicPtr<Box<dyn LogEmitter>>; crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:145-152 stores the installed emitter storage address into it after OnceLock installation.

acceptance.criterion: When no emitter is installed, log calls perform no work
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:162-164 checks the atomic pointer; crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:210-223 returns before builder/message conversion when enabled_log_emitter returns None; hardening test tracing::logging::tests::disabled_log_call_does_not_build_fields_or_message at crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:373-391 passed.

acceptance.criterion: LogEmitter: Send + Sync is required
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:64 bounds LogEmitter: Debug + Send + Sync + 'static.

issues: The original implementation used an atomic marker token rather than an atomic pointer to the installed emitter storage, so the R5 atomic-pointer criterion was only indirectly satisfied.
fixes: Changed the global fast-path state to LOG_EMITTER_PTR: AtomicPtr<Box<dyn LogEmitter>> and store ptr::from_ref(installed) after GLOBAL_LOG_EMITTER installation.
Added disabled-path regression coverage proving log_with_fields returns before converting the message or invoking the fields builder when no emitter is installed.

requirements.title: Provide structured log construction API
requirements.spec: A LogEntryBuilder SHALL provide a fluent API for constructing log entries with typed key-value fields. The builder SHALL enforce that all entries are structured: field(key, value) adds a typed field. The builder SHALL automatically capture the current trace context. The builder SHALL NOT accept format strings or free-form interpolation — message is a static or pre-formatted string, additional data goes in fields.
acceptance: LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message) creates a builder
builder.field("user_id", LogValue::String(...)) adds a structured field
builder.build() returns a LogEntry with the current trace context
No format! or write! macro usage in the builder API

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — implement `LogEntryBuilder` and its tests here
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 — builder should call `current_trace_context()` during `build()`
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:104-120 — fluent API and no format/write macro acceptance
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:31-39 — optional convenience methods can expose trace/span IDs on resulting entries
context: There are no existing builder types in `crates/liminal/src`, so choose a simple owned builder style consistent with Rust conventions.
`field("user_id", LogValue::String(...))` should add to `Vec<(String, LogValue)>`; use `key.into()` to support `&str` without requiring callers to allocate manually.
The API can accept `message: impl Into<String>` for the required `LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message)` without accepting format strings itself.
Avoid `format!` or `write!` inside the builder implementation; caller-supplied pre-formatted strings are allowed by the spec, but builder should not expose interpolation helpers.
scout.approach: Implement `pub struct LogEntryBuilder { level, target, message, fields }` with `pub fn new(level: LogLevel, target: &'static str, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self`, `pub fn field(mut self, key: impl Into<String>, value: LogValue) -> Self`, and `pub fn build(self) -> LogEntry`. `build()` should set `timestamp: Instant::now()` and `trace_context: current_trace_context()`. Add tests for fluent chaining, typed fields, automatic context capture, and absence of explicit context parameters at call sites.
scout.notes: If you later add a convenience `log(level, target, message)` function for emission, keep allocation avoidance in R7 separate: the builder itself necessarily owns `String` and `Vec`, so the filtered emission path must check level/emitter before creating a builder/entry.

files_changed.note: Added LogEntryBuilder fluent API with typed field insertion and automatic trace capture.

dev.how: Implemented `LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message)`, `.field(key, LogValue)`, and `.build()`. The builder owns only typed field values, does not provide format/interpolation helpers, and contains no `format!` or `write!` macro usage.

checklist.note: The builder API only accepts typed `LogValue` fields for additional structured data.

stories.note: Builder-created entries include trace context automatically and structured fields for log search/reconstruction.

acceptance.criterion: LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message) creates a builder
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:102-111 implements LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message).

acceptance.criterion: builder.field("user_id", LogValue::String(...)) adds a structured field
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:113-117 implements field(key, LogValue); test tracing::logging::tests::builder_constructs_structured_entries at crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:290-318 asserts String/I64/U64/F64/Bool typed fields.

acceptance.criterion: builder.build() returns a LogEntry with the current trace context
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:119-129 build() returns LogEntry and sets trace_context: capture_trace_context(); tests tracing::logging::tests::builder_captures_active_trace_context_at_build_time and builder_records_no_context_after_span_leaves_scope passed.

acceptance.criterion: No format! or write! macro usage in the builder API
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:102-129 contains no format! or write! usage; targeted search over crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs found no format!/write! matches.

requirements.title: Level-filtered emission
requirements.spec: The global log configuration SHALL support a minimum LogLevel filter. Log entries below the minimum level SHALL be discarded before constructing the LogEntry, avoiding allocation. The filter check SHALL be a single atomic load. SHALL NOT impose a default minimum level — the filter is set explicitly by the caller.
acceptance: Setting minimum level to Info causes Trace and Debug entries to be discarded
Discarded entries do not allocate a LogEntry or Vec<(String, LogValue)>
The level check is a single atomic load
No default minimum level is set — all levels are emitted until a filter is installed

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — implement atomic min-level filter and filtered emission helper
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:330-342 — existing enabled check performs an atomic load before accessing global collector
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:197-209 — existing `metrics_enabled()`/`global_registry()` fast-path shape
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:122-138 — no default minimum level and discard-before-allocation acceptance
Cargo.toml:18-27 — strict clippy may flag atomic ordering or unnecessary casts; keep implementation minimal
context: No default minimum filter means all levels should be eligible until the caller explicitly installs a minimum. Represent unset separately from `Trace`; otherwise a default `Trace` filter may technically work behaviorally but violates the explicit unset requirement.
A single atomic load can encode unset plus five levels in an `AtomicU8`, e.g. `0 = unset`, `1..=5 = Trace..Error`.

Filtering below min must happen before constructing `LogEntry`, `String`, or `Vec`; this implies a function like `log_enabled(level)` and an emission helper that takes a closure/builder only after passing checks.
The emitter-installed check and min-level filter are separate. For true no-op with no emitter, check emitter first, then min-level, then build.
scout.approach: Add `static MIN_LOG_LEVEL: AtomicU8 = AtomicU8::new(0)` and methods/functions such as `set_min_log_level(level: LogLevel)` and `min_log_level() -> Option<LogLevel>`. Implement `level_enabled(level)` with exactly one `load(Ordering::Acquire)` from the min-level atomic and compare only if nonzero. For allocation-free discard, provide an emission API like `emit_log(level, target, message, fields_fn)` or a lower-level `try_emit(builder_factory)` that checks `global_log_emitter().is_none()` and `level_enabled(level)` before constructing the builder/entry; tests can use an allocation-counting pattern if needed, but code review should ensure construction happens after checks.
scout.notes: Do not set a default minimum level during emitter installation. Also be careful not to call `message.into()` before the level/emitter checks in any public convenience logging function, because that would allocate for discarded entries.

files_changed.note: Added atomic optional minimum-level filter and lazy filtered emission helpers.

dev.how: Added an `AtomicU8` minimum-level filter with `0` representing unset, preserving no default minimum filter. `level_enabled` performs a single atomic load, and `log_with_fields` checks emitter presence and the minimum level before calling `message.into()`, allocating a builder, or allocating the entry fields vector.

checklist.note: Filtered structured logging avoids constructing typed field storage for discarded entries.

acceptance.criterion: Setting minimum level to Info causes Trace and Debug entries to be discarded
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:354-371 test tracing::logging::tests::minimum_level_filter_discards_lower_severity_entries asserts Trace/Debug disabled and Info/Warn/Error enabled; cargo test -p liminal reported it ok.

acceptance.criterion: Discarded entries do not allocate a LogEntry or Vec<(String, LogValue)>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:210-223 checks enabled_log_emitter before LogEntryBuilder::new and before fields(); hardening test tracing::logging::tests::filtered_log_call_does_not_build_fields_or_message at crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:393-412 proves filtered calls do not convert message or invoke the fields closure.

acceptance.criterion: The level check is a single atomic load
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:189-193 level_enabled performs one MIN_LOG_LEVEL.load(Ordering::Acquire) and compares against the encoded value.

acceptance.criterion: No default minimum level is set — all levels are emitted until a filter is installed
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:14 initializes MIN_LOG_LEVEL to MIN_LOG_LEVEL_UNSET; crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:16 defines unset as 0; crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:190-193 treats unset as enabled for every level; test lines 354-359 assert Trace/Debug enabled after clear.

issues: The original implementation claimed discarded entries avoided allocation, but there was no direct regression evidence that the message conversion and fields builder were skipped on disabled/filtered paths.
fixes: Added hardening tests disabled_log_call_does_not_build_fields_or_message and filtered_log_call_does_not_build_fields_or_message using a conversion probe and fields flag to verify no builder/message work occurs when logging is disabled or filtered.

requirements.title: Add logging module to tracing mod.rs
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod logging and re-export LogLevel, LogEntry, LogValue, LogEmitter, and LogEntryBuilder.
acceptance: tracing/mod.rs contains pub mod logging
LogLevel, LogEntry, LogValue, LogEmitter, LogEntryBuilder are re-exported
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1-9 — add `pub mod logging;` and re-export requested logging types while preserving mod.rs-only-declarations/re-exports rule
crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new — source of `LogLevel`, `LogEntry`, `LogValue`, `LogEmitter`, `LogEntryBuilder` for re-export
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:12-19 — crate root currently re-exports only `TraceContext`; no requirement to re-export logging at crate root
crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:1-14 — module re-export style to mirror for multi-item grouped public exports
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:140-147 — exact R8 acceptance and cargo check requirement
context: `tracing/mod.rs` currently contains only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, which matches CN3; keep it that way.
Add logging to the existing tracing namespace; do not create a top-level `logging` module in `lib.rs` unless a later brief asks for it.
The existing `pub use span::{...}` group includes functions as well as types; add a separate `pub use logging::{LogEmitter, LogEntry, LogEntryBuilder, LogLevel, LogValue};` group for clarity.
`cargo check -p liminal` must pass with Rust 2024 and all workspace lints inherited by the crate.
scout.approach: After creating `logging.rs`, edit `tracing/mod.rs` to include `pub mod logging;` with the other module declarations and add a grouped re-export for the five required public items. Leave `lib.rs` unchanged unless tests reveal consumers need crate-root exports. Run formatting and `cargo check -p liminal`; for full confidence run clippy too because the workspace denies many pedantic lints.
scout.notes: If implementation adds install/config helpers or `NoopEmitter`, consider re-exporting them too for usability, but R8 only requires the five named items; avoid over-changing public surface if acceptance tests are narrow.

files_changed.note: Declared the logging module and re-exported the required public logging types.

dev.how: Updated `tracing/mod.rs` with `pub mod logging;` and re-exported `LogEmitter`, `LogEntry`, `LogEntryBuilder`, `LogLevel`, and `LogValue` while keeping the file limited to module declarations and public re-exports.

checklist.note: The re-exported logging API exposes automatic trace-context inclusion through the tracing namespace.

acceptance.criterion: tracing/mod.rs contains pub mod logging
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1-3 contains pub mod logging.

acceptance.criterion: LogLevel, LogEntry, LogValue, LogEmitter, LogEntryBuilder are re-exported
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:5-6 re-exports LogEmitter, LogEntry, LogEntryBuilder, LogLevel, LogValue.

acceptance.criterion: cargo check -p liminal passes
acceptance.evidence: cargo check -p liminal exited 0 after hardening: Finished `dev` profile for liminal.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any metrics collection — that is OBS-001 through OBS-005.
SHALL NOT implement trace context or span types — that is OBS-006. This brief only reads the trace context.
SHALL NOT implement log output formatting (JSON, logfmt) — those are LogEmitter implementations, not the logging core.
SHALL NOT use the log crate or tracing crate — the structured logging API is self-contained.
SHALL NOT allow free-form string interpolation in the logging API — all data must be structured fields.
SHALL NOT impose a default log level filter.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
LogLevel ordering is Trace < Debug < Info < Warn < Error.
Log entries within a conversation automatically include trace_id and span_id.
Log entries outside a conversation have trace_context == None.
No Mutex or RwLock is used for trace context storage.
Logging is a no-op when no LogEmitter is installed.
